Core_Validation_Method.reason: Knockout, expression profiling, sporulation assays, and confrontation assays were used.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Wording carefully says catalytic status not established; condition-dependent key component is consistent.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: These points reflect the paper well.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Manipulation would affect sporulation and host-response tuning, as shown by mutant phenotypes.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: No such application proposal is made in the paper.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion explicitly suggests CDA3 may be regulatory rather than an active enzyme.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: As a CDA, cda6 is linked to chitosan/deacetylated chitin formation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results support roles in vegetative hyphae and early conidial maturation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper supports wall flexibility/maturation, oxidative stress protection, and mycoparasitism roles.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Vegetative/self-recognition-associated branch is consistent with Results/Discussion.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Downstream deacetylation step is consistent with CDA identity.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Involved in modification of chitin-derived wall product.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Chitin polymer as substrate is directly implied by deacetylase function.

Primary_Product.reason: Chitosan/deacetylated chitin is inferable from the enzyme class and discussion.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This description fits the reported role of deacetylating wall chitin affecting flexibility/maturation.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Cell wall-associated localization is plausible but not directly demonstrated for CDA6.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Induction by HOG, harsh wall stress, ROS, and self-recognition is supported.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Δcda6 showed altered conidial morphology, reduced chitin (66.4%), oxidative stress sensitivity as part of cda mutants, and reduced mycoparasitism.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Knockout, qRT-PCR, microscopy, GlcNAc assay, and confrontation assays were used.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Important but not clearly rate-limiting matches the paper.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This summary is consistent with Results and Discussion.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Perturbation altered sporulation quality and resilience.
